expectations.text: Creates a new config YAML with openai_text as the generation backend
Defines evaluation criteria matching the user's three requirements: click-worthiness, clarity, and length
expectations.evidence: Defined 3 criteria directly in config: click_worthiness, clarity, appropriate_length. All three match the user's stated requirements.

expectations.text: Includes realistic email subject line topics as test inputs
expectations.evidence: Created 20 diverse topics: 'SaaS launch', 'flash sale', 'newsletter', 'abandoned cart', 'cold outreach', etc.

expectations.text: Creates an initial prompt.txt as a starting point for optimization
expectations.evidence: Created initial_prompt.txt with 'basic instructions about length, clarity, and anti-spam requirements.'

expectations.text: Sets up or documents the data directory structure needed for the optimization loop
expectations.evidence: Documents full setup: 'mkdir -p /home/user/test-improver/data', 'cp initial_prompt.txt data/prompt.txt', explains get_paths() derives data paths from config location.

suggestions.reason: The without-skill run defined criteria directly in the config (3 criteria), while the with-skill run left them empty to trigger interactive generation (which would produce 5 criteria). The with-skill approach is more thorough but both pass the assertion. Consider an assertion that checks whether interactive criteria review was offered.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong execution. The without-skill run produced a complete, working config with fewer criteria but more directly.
